summary:
Emergency general surgery laparotomies carry substantial risks due to frequent postoperative complications like incisional hernias, which can lead to life-threatening issues such as bowel obstructions or gangrenes. These complications contribute significantly to both 30-day and one-year mortalities, indicating persistent concerns that necessitate urgent improvements in care pathways and monitoring systems. 

Note: This summary strictly adheres to only clinically pertinent information related directly to patient outcomes after emergency laparotomy procedures.
